item.title: Ford government’s haste in Ring of Fire road development is risky business
item.description: In expediting access to the region’s critical minerals, the province may also be accelerating environmental and financial risks

item.title: BHP’s new potash mine is a test case for Canada in how to build big
item.description: Mining giant is sinking more than $18-billion into its Jansen mine in Saskatchewan as it shifts to future-facing commodities

item.title: French-owned container ship exits Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Donald Trump renews threat to attack Iranian infrastructure

item.title: Trust fund battle shows the limits of the UK’s retail investing culture
item.description: If policymakers really want an active retail investing culture, they should remove the frictions

item.title: The hedge fund billionaire battle royale
item.description: The Two Sigma co-chief executive appointed to quell the feud between its founders quietly quit last month

item.title: UK motorists to face £2 a litre diesel within days
item.description: Scarce cargoes have boosted European diesel futures to equivalent of $211 a barrel, almost double the price of crude oil

item.title: Europe must prepare for ‘long-lasting’ energy shock, EU warns
item.description: Energy commissioner says bloc is assessing fuel rationing and releasing more oil from strategic reserves

item.title: Leading UK climate scientists warn against new North Sea drilling
item.description: Intervention comes as political pressure rises for British government response to energy crisis caused by Iran war

item.title: Credit investors flee to safety, pulling $11bn from junk bonds this year
item.description: AI disruption and war in the Middle East send market towards Treasuries and investment-grade debt

item.title: Easter eggs stay pricey even as cocoa prices plunge
item.description: Manufacturers try to recoup some losses after margins were squeezed by spike in cocoa prices

item.description: ‘Unbelievably pushy’ activist investor backed $66bn carve-out of century-old food business

item.description: Industry explores raising capital from alternative investors to cover AI mega-projects

item.title: ‘Epic’ cyclone Narelle spreads damage and disrupts mines and gas
item.description: Skies turned blood red in Western Australia ahead of tropical system that travelled for more than 6,500km

item.title: Kenya enters rare earths race with Mrima Hill deposit
item.description: Authorities seek intense competition from the US, China and Australia for ‘poster child’ project

item.title: Fund managers snap up bonds on growth threat from Iran war
item.description: After sharp market sell-off, debt investors shift focus from inflation fears to likely damage to economies

item.title: Prediction markets: the hunt for the new ‘dumb money’
item.description: As new bettors flock to platforms such as Kalshi and Polymarket, some make easy prey for trading firms and professional gamblers

item.title: How US anti-vaxxers found they were not immune to pushback
item.description: Recent setbacks for the movement signal a potential turning point for the vaccine makers as the market picks up

item.title: Robert Armstrong on coining the Taco trade
item.description: Markets went haywire after the ‘liberation day’ tariffs. Until investors started noticing a pattern

item.title: U.S. government sues three states over efforts to regulate prediction markets
item.description: Connecticut, Arizona and Illinois have accused companies such as Polymarket of engaging in illegal online gambling under state law

item.title: Oil prices continue to climb and could surge to US$150 if access to Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted
item.description: Brent crude has climbed 49 per cent since the war in Iran began

item.title: Oil cargo prices surge as fears of supply shortage grip market
item.description: Address by Trump signalling possible escalation of Iran conflict raises prospect of prolonged closure of Strait of Hormuz

item.title: The gas price shock isn’t hitting Canadian cities evenly
item.description: Cities that had higher prices from the start – particularly Vancouver and Victoria – experienced smaller price shocks

item.title: Ottawa, drug companies launch task force to prepare for USMCA talks
item.description: Group plans to meet every two weeks and prepare a report in 90 days with options for Canada’s response

item.title: M&S demands urgent action by ministers to tackle ‘brazen’ UK shoplifting
item.description: FTSE 100 retailer urges the government and London mayor to better support the police

item.title: Nicola Wealth halts investor redemptions on real estate value fund, plans to wind it down
item.description: Fund is latest to curtail redemptions as investors sour on private markets

item.title: Trump has no Plan B in Iran. He never had much of a Plan A
item.description: The U.S. President took to the airwaves for what was little more than a rant about old grievances and supposed achievements

item.title: Corporate takeovers fell in first quarter as geopolitics affected deal-making
item.description: Experts say continuing violent global conflicts are having an impact on the market

item.title: Starbucks moves to weekly pay for all U.S. baristas, adds more tips and bonuses
item.description: New bonus structure would let baristas and shift supervisors can earn up to an additional $1,200 per year

item.title: Willie Walsh is that rarity in India — a foreign chief executive
item.description: While Indians run some of the world’s biggest finance and tech companies, the country’s own aviation sector needs to import talent

item.title: Crypto exchange Coinbase gets conditional U.S. approval for trust company charter
item.description: Full approval would let top U.S. cryptocurrency exchange operate as a federally regulated crypto custodian

item.title: Oil prices are soaring, and so are fixed mortgage rates. Should you go variable?
item.description: Anyone taking out a floating rate must have the risk tolerance and budget padding to absorb potentially higher payments

item.title: Middle East war day 34 as it happened: Oil jumps after Donald Trump’s remarks dash hopes of end to war, Macron says ‘unrealistic’ to think strait of Hormuz could be opened by military operation,
item.description: West Texas Intermediate settles at highest since mid-2022 on US president’s threat to hit Iran ‘extremely hard’

item.title: Industry Minister rejects Stellantis plan to build Chinese EVs at idled Brampton plant
item.description: Mélanie Joly says production at plant must be supported by Ontario and Unifor union

item.title: Canadian regulators considering tighter rules on prediction markets
item.description: Regulators have largely restricted Canadians’ access to the markets, which let users bet on real-world events

item.description: AI-related worries are driving an investor exodus from private credit firm’s technology-focused fund

item.description: Tech showman seeks to tap the Fomo factor with a market sales pitch for the ages

item.title: Hackers steal $280mn from decentralised finance crypto exchange Drift
item.description: Loss equates to about half of total US dollar value on deposit with platform popular for trading perpetual derivatives

item.title: Government-run grocery stores: A leftist idea coming from the heart, not the head
item.description: Even with all the taxpayer money needed to run such a scheme, it’s a fantasy to believe it could meaningfully address affordability issues

item.description: Country posted a services trade surplus of $400-million as exports rose 1.5%, Statscan says
